paragraphs.body: December 11th 2024. Unfortunately, I will never forget this day and it lives in my mind rent free at all times. Although on the outside, writing a whole essay on my iPad might seem a little silly. It’s not for me. During this treacherous year my iPad was the only constant in my life. It was more constant than my friends, family, coaches, and my sport. At a time when things felt so out of control for me this iPad helped me feel like I did have some control in my life. My iPad represents my short lived athletic career at the University of Pittsburgh. I got my iPad for being a student athlete here at the University of Pittsburgh. This is important to me because I felt special. I thought “wow the school cared about me enough to try to make my life easier and acknowledge the hard work I’ve put in to get here enough to get me an iPad!” I would do anything to feel appreciated like that athletically now, and now unfortunately all I hear is how not good I am.

It was a usual cold December day. The Morale was very high for the track team because we just had to get through this week and then we were able to go home for Christmas break, and when we would return it would be time for track season. Which is the fun part of track when you finally get to race and are not just running around the track like a hamster on a training wheel. It was a Tuesday, so I knew this workout was going to be hard but I was ready to take the challenge. In the fieldhouse there is a small track about 3 ½ feet wide, and it’s a little over 200 meters. Because the fieldhouse is so small and it's right next to the fuel station which is where athletes can get snacks and meals after their training. And because of this for some reason it always smelled like tuna salad. Not a good thing to smell during a hard workout.

The workout was 350,250,200,150,150,150 if you are not a track nerd like me, just know this was a hard workout. After we finish my coach says “After you cool down, come up to my office. ” Not a sentence any freshman wants to hear. I’m going through everything in my head that could have possibly been wrong, I could have never expected what was to come... I go to his office basically shaking. He says “after some consideration and talking to the coaches, I have decided it would be better if you were a thrower. ” My heart sank to my stomach. I was taken aback “a thrower?” I say try not to sound too disrespectful. In my mind I was thinking “I’m the only freshman who made it to the finals last meet!” I’ve had one opportunity to prove myself and I did it! Plus I had never done anything but sprinting events on track. He says some other things but I black out. I left that conversation with a smile on my face and saying thank you, because I didn’t want him to be angry. I came down the stairs trying to control my laughter but I simply could not. Two of my teammates saw me coming from his office laughing asking “what happened?” I told them the news and that’s when things became real quickly!

We go into the locker room and that’s when I’m hit with the emotion I could not process earlier. My running career was simply over at Pitt. Nobody cared, people I thought were my friends said I should be grateful for this opportunity and just left me there. I was never supposed to get cut, I thought. I proved myself. Despite my mental health being the worst it has ever been in my last 18 years of life. I had to dust myself off, pick up my iPad and continue to study because this all happened during finals week. Despite going through a long period of uncertainty, not even sure what the next day would hold for me and my life being flipped upside down, my ipad was there, ready with my schoolwork. Because even though my life was a mess and I felt like I was drowning. My iPad did not care because I needed to get good grades, and school didn’t stop, so neither did my iPad.

A competitive person has nothing else in their mind except to complete the goal. If they are running a race the competitive runner takes giant strides, and moves their legs very fast. A competitive runner comes down on their feet as light as a feather. Their feet are barely on the ground; it nearly looks like they are flying like a peregrine falcon. A competitive runner does not like to lose. A competitive runner does not focus on what others are doing. A competitive runner does tune out all distractions around them. A competitive runner does have superstitions and rituals the night before the race. A competitive runner does much more than run, you know? A competitive runner has to look at all the runners stats the night before. She also has to make sure her hair is perfectly slicked into a high bun. She also has to make sure she has her favorite pearl studs in, never hoops because it’s too distracting. She also has to roll out on a foam roller for at least 15 minutes the night before. She has to bake the most delicious 2 dozen chocolate chip cookies the night before a race for her family to eat.This competitive runner has a goal to be the absolute best and won’t stop until she gets there. Being competitive adds so much joy to my life, and despite still being on the team and competing, it was not the same as running and unfortunately never satisfied the itch I have to be competitive.

annotations.comment: This definition paragraph is a fabulous addition. It clarifies so much about what's at the heart of this essay (which is you, your heart, your sense of self, your identity)

paragraphs.body: This iPad made my self confidence as a person go up and it also has watched my confidence and self worth go down. My iPad when I use it in the morning to try and get ahead of my work is cold from sitting in my big empty bag for over 12 hours. I have really weird sensory issues when it comes to my electronics. And I can’t have anything on them, especially my iPad. My iPad has a big grey screen, I think it’s a 12 inch wide iPad. And I protect mine with a white case, and sometimes I have a magnetic keyboard that I take on and off. It is very light even though it carries all that I need for my six classes, and 18 credit hours this semester. On my iPad I have google docs and words on there, which hold all my papers for all my classes. I also have canvas on my iPad which lets me know what assignments I have due for which class. I also have an outlook email on there. This helps me keep in touch with all of my professors. And finally I let myself have one “fun” app on here, and that’s TikTok.. Sometimes if my phone dies and I need a speaker, I’ll use my iPad as a speaker to play music and it is 10x louder than my iPhone. Despite no longer technically being a student-athlete here, my iPad makes me feel like I am because I have the same iPad as the thousands of other student athletes here. And it reminds me that I once was and helps me remember how I felt and how happy I was at the beginning of the year.

And although this iPad has gotten me through arguably the hardest year of my life, I think I am ready to pack it up. I am trying to focus on healing what happened to me this year, and I think that means the last step is putting the iPad away. Because despite my iPad being the only good thing to come away with me from Pitt this year, it’s still a part of Pitt and a time I do not want to remember. And every time I look at this iPad I’m reminded of all of the pain, and suffering I went through this year. In conclusion this iPad is not an odd object to me at all. My iPad has been a lifeline for me this year that I will forever appreciate and cherish. I will always remember my University of Pittsburgh 2024-2025 freshman year iPad, and will forever and always be eternally grateful for a small black screen for making my life feel stable in a time full of inconsistency.

annotations.comment: So many great ideas emerging in this essay-- Great voice, too. I'm so interested to find out more about the metaphor you're developing ,and I'm drawn to so many places of heat and energy. On that note: who is this essay's audience? Purpose? Also: what is this essay's "word"? I think that will also be revealing. Well done! Also, the definition paragraph you're missing is actually the paragraph you turned in on your "descriptive" paragraph homework activity. Idefinitely think you should include it--it points directly to the idea that you _will_ win and you _will_ reach your goals despite yourexperience. This is an awesome essay! It has so much range and depth. It takes your reader on a kind of journey through anger and confusion tohealing, which is powerful and insightful. Great job with the definition paragraph as well. It's so good :)
